title: Einsam
bio:
MUSIK
„Gänsehaut-Pop mit ganz viel Gefühl“, so beschreibt Deutschlands größter Musikblog TONSPION die Musik von CHIOPS.
Mit großer Kreativität lässt der Chemnitzer Sänger elektronische Sounds mit Gitarren verschmelzen und kreiert damit eine Deutschpopvariante, die sich von der Masse abhebt. 
In seinen Liedern erzählt der Sänger & Songwriter auf einfühlsame Weise persönliche Geschichten in denen sich viele Menschen wiederfinden und erzeugt dabei Emotionen die unter die Haut gehen.

title: Reeperbahn 2011 (What it's like) [feat. Jan Delay]
album: Album: MTV Unplugged - Live Aus Dem Hotel Atlantic (Deluxe Version)
bio: Lindenberg (born May 17, 1946 in Gronau) started his musical career as a drummer. In 1969 Lindenberg founded his first band Free Orbit and also appeared as a studio and guest musician (with Michael Naura, Knut Kiesewetter). In 1970 he collaborated as a drummer with jazz-saxophonist Klaus Doldinger in Munich. In 1971, a band founded by Lindenberg, Passport put out its first album, with Lindenberg on drums. He also played drums for the theme music for the German TV series Tatort.

title: so schön
bio:
There are multiple artists named Plastik:

1) Plastik is a trancey German band. 

2) Plastik are a three piece from London, comprising of Gareth Moss (vocals, guitar, keyboards), Andy Natt (bass, backing vocals) & Paul Cox (drums, backing vocals). They are now a 4 piece called God Love You For A Liar.

3) Plastik is an alternative rock band from Vaala, Finalnd. They are remembered by songs like "Vivid Dream", "Mornings" or "Warp Overtaking Me". Plastik included Markus R.

title: Borderline Betty
album: Album: Poet der Affen / Poet of the Apes
bio: "Es ist alles was ich liebe, alles was ich kann, alles was ich immer wollte und alles, was ich immer machen werde: Popmusik die noch so klingt wie sie war, bevor sie von den Plattenfirmen versaut wurde. Aufgewachsen bin ich mit John, Paul, George und Ringo, Miles Davis und Leonard Cohen. Später trauerte ich mit Robert Smith und seinen Cure um die Wette, übte Chorsatz mit Police und lernte Pöbeln bei den Brüdern Gallagher. Rio hat mir Deutschunterricht gegeben.

title: Kein Mann für eine Nacht - Radio Edit
bio:
Fabyán, ein Newcomer in der Schlagerwelt !

Am 6. August 2021 hatte Fabyán seine Debüt Single 
“Kein Mann für eine Nacht” veröffentlicht. Der Song wurde von Lucas Cordalis getextet und produziert. Es war ein lang ersehnter Wunsch von Fabyán einmal selber einen Schlager Song zu singen. Denn Fabyán ist mit den Schlager Songs aus den 70´  groß geworden. Bis heute hört und liebt er den Schlager. Auch wenn er noch in anderen “Genres” wie Chill out

title: Feld aus Gold
bio:
Dirk Patrick Michaelis (* 26. November 1961 in Karl-Marx-Stadt) ist ein deutscher Sänger und Komponist.
Von 1984 bis 1991 war er der Frontmann der DDR-Rockband Karussell. Als sein bekanntestes Lied gilt „Als ich fortging“.
Nachdem er eine Ausbildung zum Maurer anfing, besuchte er Unterrichtsstunden in der Musikschule und reiste seit 1980 als Amateurmusiker durch die Republik (DDR). Seinen Lebensunterhalt bestritt er in dieser Zeit unter anderem als Krankenpfleger und Kindergärtner.

title: November
bio:
Juli (German for "July") is a German alternative pop band from Giessen, Hesse. The band was formed in 2001 from the earlier group Sunnyglade and consists of singer Eva Briegel, guitarists Jonas Pfetzing and Simon Triebel, bassist Andreas "Dedi" Herde and drummer Marcel Römer.

The group originated in 1996 under the name Sunnyglade. The original line-up consisted of Andreas "Dedi" Herde, Martin Möller, Jonas Pfetzing, Marcel Römer and Simon Triebel, who also served as lead vocalist.

title: Auch im Regen
bio:
Rosenstolz was a German pop duo from Berlin, active from 1991 to 2012, consisting of singer AnNa R. and musician Peter Plate. The duo achieved chart success in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland, with five of their studio albums reaching number one on the German albums chart. They split up to pursue individual music projects but did not rule out a future reunion. AnNa R. died in March 2025.

Rosenstolz's music incorporated elements of pop, chanson, and rock, with lyrics often focusing on themes of love, sex, and emotions.

title: Nie vergessen
bio:
Glasperlenspiel, named after the novel "Das Glasperlenspiel" by Hermann Hesse, is a German Electro Pop Duo from Stockach am Bodensee (Baden-Württemberg). Formed in 2010 by Carolin Niemczyk (*1990 in Singen/Hohentwiel) and Daniel Grunenberg (*November 8, 1988 in Stockach).

Their debut single "Echt" was released on September 9, 2011 and their debut album "Beweg dich mit mir" followed on September 30, 2011. Glasperlenspiel participated on September 29, 2011 at the Bundesvision Song Contest 2011 and reached the 4th place with Echt.

title: Vaterland
bio: Konstantin Alexander Wecker (born in Munich 01.06.47) is a German singer/songwriter with piano as his main instrument, widely known for his political engagement. He rose to fame in the seventies, with "Willy" (on the album "Genug ist nicht genug" ["enough is not enough"] in 1977) as his first big hit. He has released more than 40 albums, tours extensively, also with Hannes Wader, and also enjoys a carreer as actor (infamous for the sexfilms of the seventies), author (often self-biographical in character) and composer of film-music and musicals.

title: Hearts In Heaven
bio: Matthias Reim (born on 26 November 1957 in Korbach, Hesse) is a German pop/rock singer-songwriter. Performing an often dramatic, emotional form of 'schlager' music, his first single, titled "Verdammt, ich lieb’ Dich" (English: "Damn, I love you"), came out in 1990. The internationally famous single sold over 2.5 million copies and was at top of the charts in Germany for 16 weeks. No single had kept that position for so long since 1971, with Reim sharing this distinction with Boney M.

title: SAG MIR
album: Album: GIB MIR ETWAS WORAN ICH MICH
bio: ’All I Want’ is the emotion-charged debut single from German singer-producer Ron Flieger written during his latest summer stay in Los Angeles. It features his own vocal performance along with female shouts by a L.A.-based friend of Ron. Alysha Nett is starring the cover photo. After reaching upwards of 35.000 streams in just a few short weeks, Radio stations like FluxFM (Winner Best Radio Station Worldwide 2014) & Sveriges Radio P3 added this debut track to their playlists.

1. Bosse - a German rock band from Braunschweig (Brunswick).
Bosse got their name by their singer and former Hyperchild-member Axel Bosse. Further members of Bosse are Björn Krüger (drums), Thorsten Sala (guitar) und Theofilos Fotiades (bass).
In 2005 the band released their first single Kraft, produced by Wolfgang Stach.

title: Du Musst Dahin, Wo's Weh Tut
bio:
Virginia Jetzt! was a German indie pop band founded in the small town of Elsterwerda in Brandenburg in 1999. In May 2010 the band announced break-up for the end of the year. 

The band released four albums: Their 2003 debut album "Wer hat Angst vor Virginia Jetzt?" - a play on words from the Edward Albee play "Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?" - was followed by "Anfänger" in 2004, "Land Unter" in 2007 and their last one "Blühende Landschaften" in 2009.

title: ganz laut
bio:
Erst sind es nur Gitarren die einem ein Gefühl von Leichtigkeit geben und ohne das man es merkt baut sich ein Song auf, der einen mitnimmt, auf eine Reise voller Bilder von Weite, einer Prise Sommer mit einem winzigen Hauch von Melancholie.
Aufrechte, wahre, unverschönte, klare & direkte Songs, die ins Ohr gehen und gleich noch einmal gehört werden wollen.

"... ich hab dann meine Gitarre genommen und einfach losgesungen, von den kleinen Dingen, die oft ganz groß sind und den Bildern, die raus wollen.

At this time Locas In Love consists of 4 Members: Jan Niklas Jansen is playing guitar (sometimes he is singing too), Maurizio Arca on drums, Stefanie Schrank as bass player and Singer, Bjoern Sonnenberg at guitar and on vocals. Locas in Love have released  4 EPs and in 2004 their first full length record so far. The Band is named after the after a comic series of Bros Hernandez.

title: Berge versetzen
bio: Klee is a German Pop-band from Cologne. Their musical influences however, are mainly from British bands like New Order, The Cure, The Smiths, and The Beatles. In 1996 Suzie Kerstgens, Tom Deininger and Sten Servaes founded the band Ralley, but an accident  forced them to take a break and reform as Klee in 2002. The name was inspired (according to Suzie Kerstgens) by the work of Paul Klee, and also refers to the four leaf clover as a good luck symbol.

Astra Kid was a indie rock group from Germany. The group was founded in the town Datteln in the Ruhr Area around 1996. The group members were Stefan "Pele" Götzer (vocals, guitar), Christian Götzer (guitar, vocals), André Raschke (bass guitar, vocals) und Marc Baumann (drums). The group dissolved in 2005. And played in the spring of 2006 their last two (sold-out) concerts in Essen. Both brothers still working in a new project together their new band called Autobahn.

title: Wenn Man Im Atmen Innehält
bio:
Kante is a band from Hamburg, Germany which was founded in 1995. Their music is basically a mix of "Hamburger Schule" and Jazz with a few Pop-, Post-, and Indie-Rock elements. Their lyrics are mostly melancholic and thought-provoking.
Before their fifth album "Die Tiere sind unruhig" Kante was nearly about to split up as their way of making music has become too outstanding and explicit. Their new album though is much more vital and has filled them again with joy and fun making music.

title: Hommage
bio:
Anajo is an indie band from Augsburg, Germany. Its members are Oliver Gottwald (guitar and lyrics), Michael Schmidt (bass guitar) and Ingolf Nössner (drums). The band was founded in early 1999 and after several demo records they released their first album in 2004 named "Nah bei mir". The band name is attributed to a broken television where the name of the Bud Spencer movie "Banana Joe" was barely readable.

A first taste of their second album "Hallo

title: Der Arme Graue Kater
album: Album: Es Ist Wie Es Ist
bio: Pur is a German pop band from Bietigheim-Bissingen. The group was initially founded in 1975 under the name Crusade by Roland Bless and Ingo Reidl. Their first releases came out under the name Opus, but after an Austrian band with the same name had a huge hit single in Germany in 1985, they switched to the name Pur. Pur's first hit single in Germany was "Lena", released in 1990. In the 1990s and 2000s they had a string of number 1 albums in Germany. The producer of Pur was Dieter Falk from 1990 to 1998.

title: Du
bio:
MIA. (alternate spelling: Mia.) is a German rock/pop band from Berlin.

MIA. originally formed in 1997 when TV host Sarah Kuttner introduced schoolmates Mieze and Andy Ross to Robert Schütze and Ingo Puls. They took on Hannes Schulze as drummer and one year later the band, then called Me in Affairs signed a contract with independent music label R.O.T. - Respect or Tolerate. They soon abbreviated their band name to MIA., but now claim the letters do no longer stand just for Me in Affairs

title: Es Muss Nur Weit Sein
bio:
Toni Kater ist der Künstlername von Anett Ecklebe. Sie wurde 1977 in Blankenburg (Harz) geboren, wuchs im elterlichen Haus mit klassischer Musik auf und erlernte dort das Klavierspielen.

1993 verließ sie mit ihrer besten Freundin das Elternhaus nach Ibiza. Ein halbes Jahr später kam sie zurück und besuchte das Internat Wettin, wo sie ihren Schulabschluss erlangte.

Anschließend wohnte sie in Halle (Saale).

Nach dem 1. Plattenvertrag zog sie 1996 nach Berlin.

bio: Nordlicht is a black metal / ambient solo project of Nimosh from Switzerland. He also runs Kunsthall Productions label with Wintherr from Paysage d'Hiver. Nordlicht was formed already in 1996 though to this day only one release has been released - the debut album "Nebelmeer" in 2002. title: Das optimale Leben
album: Album: Das optimale Leben - Tour Edition (Audio Version)
bio: Annett Louisan (born Annett Päge; 2 April 1977 in Havelberg, Saxony-Anhalt, East Germany) is a German singer. She lives in Hamburg, Germany. Louisan is her stage name, derived from the name of her grandmother, Louise. Concerning her birth year, there is conflicting testimony, with the official date being given as 1979. Her first album, Bohème, was listed on German charts for almost a year, with a peak ranking of third place. She appeared on the AVO Session program at Basle on 7 November 2006.

title: Land Unter (Live)
bio:
Herbert Arthur Wiglev Clamor Grönemeyer (born 12 April 1956) is a German singer, musician, producer, composer, and actor, known for his popularity in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland.

Grönemeyer was born in Göttingen and spent most of his childhood and youth in Bochum. He began piano lessons at the age of eight, which later contributed to his work as a pianist and composer at Schauspielhaus Bochum. In 1979, he appeared as Lorenzo in "The Merchant of Venice" at Schauspielhaus Köln and began acting in television productions.

Louisan is her stage name, derived from the name of her grandmother, Louise. Concerning her birth year, there is conflicting testimony, with the official date being given as 1979. Her first album, Bohème, was listed on German charts for almost a year, with a peak ranking of third place. She appeared on the AVO Session program at Basle on 7 November 2006.

title: Besser so
bio: Astra Kid was a indie rock group from Germany. The group was founded in the town Datteln in the Ruhr Area around 1996. The group members were Stefan "Pele" Götzer (vocals, guitar), Christian Götzer (guitar, vocals), André Raschke (bass guitar, vocals) und Marc Baumann (drums). The group dissolved in 2005. And played in the spring of 2006 their last two (sold-out) concerts in Essen. Both brothers still working in a new project together their new band called Autobahn.

album: Album: Es war nicht alles schlecht
bio: Die Prinzen are a German (sometimes a capella) pop band from Leipzig, Germany. Four of the seven band members sang in the famous "Thomanerchor" (St. Thomas Boys Choir) in Leipzig. Wolfgang Lenk (tenor) and Sebastian Krumbiegel (tenor) attended the same school in Leipzig and founded a band called "Phoenix." This band became "Herzbuben" in 1987. Later, Jens Sembdner (bass) joined the band. In 1989, Henri Schmidt (baritone) became a member of "Herzbuben".

title: 100 Flieger
bio: Luttenberger*Klug is a duo of the two young Austrian girls from Styria: Chrissi Klug and Michelle Luttenberger. Their first single, "Super Sommer", had much less success than their later single, "Vergiss mich" which also came with their debut album, "Mach dich bereit".  "Mach dich bereit" has been available worldwide since 2 March 2007. Their latest album, "Mädchen im Regen", was released in Europe late 2008 with much waited enthusiasm and had a worldwide release in 2009 as its popularity soared in English speaking countries.

title: Unerwartet (Ein Fenster zum Himmel)
bio:
Rosenstolz was a German pop duo from Berlin, active from 1991 to 2012, consisting of singer AnNa R. and musician Peter Plate. The duo achieved chart success in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland, with five of their studio albums reaching number one on the German albums chart. They split up to pursue individual music projects but did not rule out a future reunion. AnNa R. died in March 2025.

Rosenstolz's music incorporated elements of pop, chanson, and rock, with lyrics often focusing on themes of love, sex, and emotions.

bio: Locas In Love is a german music group from Cologne. Formed in 2001 as musical project of Bjoern Sonnenberg and Stefanie Schrank they had several line-ups. At this time Locas In Love consists of 4 Members: Jan Niklas Jansen is playing guitar (sometimes he is singing too), Maurizio Arca on drums, Stefanie Schrank as bass player and Singer, Bjoern Sonnenberg at guitar and on vocals. Locas in Love have released  4 EPs and in 2004 their first full length record so far. The Band is named after the after a comic series of Bros Hernandez.

title: Zombi
bio:
Kante is a band from Hamburg, Germany which was founded in 1995. Their music is basically a mix of "Hamburger Schule" and Jazz with a few Pop-, Post-, and Indie-Rock elements. Their lyrics are mostly melancholic and thought-provoking.
Before their fifth album "Die Tiere sind unruhig" Kante was nearly about to split up as their way of making music has become too outstanding and explicit. Their new album though is much more vital and has filled them again with joy and fun making music.

bio:
Anajo is an indie band from Augsburg, Germany. Its members are Oliver Gottwald (guitar and lyrics), Michael Schmidt (bass guitar) and Ingolf Nössner (drums). The band was founded in early 1999 and after several demo records they released their first album in 2004 named "Nah bei mir". The band name is attributed to a broken television where the name of the Bud Spencer movie "Banana Joe" was barely readable.

title: Tausend Menschen
bio:
Johannes Oerding (born 26 December 1981 in Münster) is a German pop singer and songwriter.

Oerding was born in Münster in the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia and grew up in Geldern-Kapellen on the Lower Rhine with two brothers and two sisters. His father worked as a general practitioner and was also involved in local historical research and the scouting movement, receiving the Federal Cross of Merit for his civic engagement. Oerding attended the Friedrich-Spee-Gymnasium in Geldern, where he completed his Abitur.

title: Liebeslieder
bio:
Virginia Jetzt! was a German indie pop band founded in the small town of Elsterwerda in Brandenburg in 1999. In May 2010 the band announced break-up for the end of the year. 

The band released four albums: Their 2003 debut album "Wer hat Angst vor Virginia Jetzt?" - a play on words from the Edward Albee play "Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?" - was followed by "Anfänger" in 2004, "Land Unter" in 2007 and their last one "Blühende Landschaften" in 2009.

title: Dein Cabaret ist tot
bio:
1) Silly is a German rock band. Founded in East Berlin in 1978, Silly was one of the country's most popular music acts, and was well known for its charismatic lead singer Tamara Danz, who died on 22 July 1996. The German actress and singer Anna Loos took over permanently the role of the lead singer in fall 2006 with remarkable success. Official Website

2) Silly was a pop punk band from Banner Elk, NC. They released a a demo cassette entitled "Silly Doesn't Do It" and a CD entitled "Will You Go With Me?" in 1995 on Blindside/Ghostmeat Records.

title: Wir Sind Eins
bio:
Tom Albrecht writes, produces, sings. Inexhaustibly and with artistic consequence. An individual understanding of the world emerges out of a sensitive and couragous view on everything around and inside Tom. 
To translate this valuable insight into music and lyrics and to realize it, is the goal of this all. Tom is able to wish the world a Schoenen Tag cheerfully and at the same time spread staggering melancholy.

Tom Albrecht writes many songs for different artists. Christina Stürmer for example.

title: Falschrum
bio:
There are at least four artists using this name:

1) Tele (テレ)  is the solo project of Japanese musician 谷口喜多朗 (Kitarou Taniguchi; born June 1, 2000) who started his activities in 2022 . 

He writes, composes, and arranges all of the music himself and is signed to the label Toys Factory and his agency is RED. The music can be described as indie-rock, soft rock and rock with strong pop influences.

The name “Tele” comes from Fender’s Telecaster electric guitar.

title: Nachbarschaft
bio:
There are three bands known by the name Echt:

1. Echt was a German pop band from Flensburg, active between 1997 and 2002. The band gained significant popularity in Germany, with their second album, "Freischwimmer", reaching number one on the German album charts in 1999.

2. ECHT! is a jazz band based in Brussels. The name "ECHT!" translates to "vrai de vrai" (real thing) in Brusseleir, a Brabantian dialect spoken in Brussels.

3. Echt was a Dutch blues rock band active in the 1990s.

title: Ich lass ein Licht an für dich
bio: Klee is a German Pop-band from Cologne. Their musical influences however, are mainly from British bands like New Order, The Cure, The Smiths, and The Beatles. In 1996 Suzie Kerstgens, Tom Deininger and Sten Servaes founded the band Ralley, but an accident  forced them to take a break and reform as Klee in 2002. The name was inspired (according to Suzie Kerstgens) by the work of Paul Klee, and also refers to the four leaf clover as a good luck symbol.

bio: "Fertig, Los!" is a german pop band from Munich. The four young members (Flo,Julia, Raphael, Philipp) share their love for poetry and pop-music, so they formed a band which was originally called "spunk". As soon as they realized that there are over a dozen bands called "spunk" in southern germany alone, they renamed themselves to "Fertig, Los!".

title: Lara (Goes Motown live)
bio: Kim Alexander Frank is a German singer and actor, born on May 24th, 1982 in Flensburg, Schleswig Holstein, Germany. Frank started his career at the age of 16 as the front man of the pop band Echt. After five glorious years the band split up and Frank started working on his solo album "Hellblau", mainly produced in a garage by the guitarist and bassist of Echt. The fans had to wait for a long time, but since february 2007 Frank is back in the German charts.

title: Blau im Blau
bio:
Catterfeld was born in Erfurt, Thuringia on December 2, 1979. At the age of 15 she started playing the piano and the flute, simultaneously taking first dance and vocal lessons. After her high school graduation she studied Pop and Jazz at the music academy in Leipzig for two years. In 1998 she released her first recordings under the stage name KIV.

After her studies Yvonne participated in the singing contest Stimme 2000 (briefly performing under the name Vivianne)

Wir sind Helden (German for "We Are Heroes") was a German pop rock band established in 2000 in Hamburg and later based in Berlin. The band consisted of Judith Holofernes (lead vocals, guitar), Pola Roy (drums), Mark Tavassol (bass), and Jean-Michel Tourette (keyboard, guitar).

The band gained wider attention in 2002 with the song "Guten Tag", which received radio airplay and television exposure despite the absence of a recording contract. Their debut album "Die Reklamation"

bio:
Muff Potter is a german speaking punk band from Rheine and Münster, Northrhine-Westphalia, Germany. They disbanded in 2009 and reunited again in 2018.

Formed in 1993 they are named by the Vagrant from Mark Twain's "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer".

They define their music style as angry pop music, influenced by bands like Slime and Tocotronic.

Bandmembers:
vocals/guitar: Thorsten "Nagel" Nagelschmidt
vocals/guitar: Dennis Scheider
bass: Dominic "Shredder" Laurenz

title: Zeig mir wie du tanzt
bio: Frida Gold is a German band from Hattingen in the south of the Ruhr region in Germany, which in various guises has been active since 2007. Frida Gold primarily performs music in the dance-pop genre with most of the tracks / lyrics being in German. The band is currently made up of: Alina Süggeler (lead singer), Andreas "Andi" Weizel (bass), Julian Cassel (guitar) and Thomas "Tommy" Holtgreve (drums). The songs are primarily written by the singer Alina Süggeler and bassist Andi Weizel.

title: Sozialer Brennpunkt Ich
bio: ClickClickDecker (AKA Kevin Hamann), is a Singer-Songwriter from Hamburg, Germany. He originally exclusively released his music on tape, but since 2002 he also offers CDs. In 2006 his third CD, called "Nichts für Ungut", reached stores. Everything he does is based on the DIY-attitude.

title: Es Regnet
bio: Element of Crime was founded 1985 in West-Berlin, Germany by Sven Regener, Jakob Ilja Friderichs, Paul Lukas, Jürgen Fabritius and Uwe Bauer. After 4 recordings in English, Sven Regener decided to write all his lyrics in German. Their first German song Der Mann vom Gericht however appeared on their album The Ballad of Jimmy & Johnny (1989). After Damals Hinterm Mond was released in 1991, the band has gotten more and more known, but still remained underground.

title: Süchtig
bio:
Juli (German for "July") is a German alternative pop band from Giessen, Hesse. The band was formed in 2001 from the earlier group Sunnyglade and consists of singer Eva Briegel, guitarists Jonas Pfetzing and Simon Triebel, bassist Andreas "Dedi" Herde and drummer Marcel Römer.

The group originated in 1996 under the name Sunnyglade. The original line-up consisted of Andreas "Dedi" Herde, Martin Möller, Jonas Pfetzing, Marcel Römer and Simon Triebel, who also served as lead vocalist.

Polarkreis 18 is a German pop band from Dresden, Saxony. The musical style of the band can be described as pop, synthpop and indietronic. The bandmembers Felix Räuber (vocals), Philipp Makolies (guitar, piano), Christian Grochau (drums), Uwe Pasora (bass), Bernhard Silvester Wenzel (piano, electronics, backingvocals) and Ludwig Bauer (piano, guitar, trumpet) met at school where they formed the band Jack of all Trades in 1998. In 2004 they renamed their band Polarkreis 18.

bio:
MIA. (alternate spelling: Mia.) is a German rock/pop band from Berlin.

MIA. originally formed in 1997 when TV host Sarah Kuttner introduced schoolmates Mieze and Andy Ross to Robert Schütze and Ingo Puls. They took on Hannes Schulze as drummer and one year later the band, then called Me in Affairs signed a contract with independent music label R.O.T. - Respect or Tolerate. They soon abbreviated their band name to MIA., but now claim the letters do no longer stand just for Me in Affairs

title: Atemlos durch die Nacht
bio: Helene Fischer (born 5 August 1984) is a German singer and entertainer. Since her debut in 2005 she has won numerous awards, including 17 Echo awards, 4 "Die Krone der Volksmusik" awards and 3 Bambi awards. According to record certifications she has sold at least 15,000,000 albums (As of 11/18). In June 2014, her multi platinum 2013 album "Farbenspiel" became the most legally downloaded album by a German artist of all time, and her 2017 album "Helene Fischer (2017)" became the 2nd highest first week sales debut ever on the German Albums Charts.

title: Wahnsinn
bio:
Wolfgang "Wolle" Petry (born Franz Hubert Wolfgang Remling, 22 September 1951) is a German schlager musician and songwriter from Cologne, Germany. In 1997, he was recognized as the leading German-language musician in terms of chart performance for that year, largely due to the success of his album "Alles".

Petry received the Echo award for "Best Folk/Pop Artist National/International" consecutively from 1997 to 2001. He was also awarded the Goldene Stimmgabel for Most Successful German Solo Pop Act in 1996

title: Verlieben, Verloren, Vergessen, Verzeih'n
bio:

title: Er gehört zu mir
bio:
Marianne Rosenberg (born March 10, 1955, in West Berlin) is a German pop and schlager singer as well as a songwriter. She gained fame in the 1970s with hits like Mr. Paul McCartney, Er gehört zu mir, Lieder der Nacht, and Marleen. Rosenberg was born into an artistic family in Berlin and had a notable relationship with Ilja Richter in the late 1970s. She has a son, Max (born 1993), who is also a musician.

Rosenberg's father, Otto Rosenberg, was a Sinto and a survivor of the Porajmos, the genocide of the European Roma and Sinti during WWII.

title: Guten Morgen Sonnenschein
bio: Nana Mouskouri (Greek: Νάνα Μούσχουρη, born Ioanna Mouskouri at October 13, 1934, in Chania, Crete, Greece) is a Greek singer and politician. She was known as Nana to her friends and family as a child. She is the world's highest-selling female recording artist, having sold over 300 million albums in fifteen languages, including Greek, French, English, Spanish, Italian, German, Latin, and Portuguese, over 450 albums. She is France's number one selling artist.

title: Ich liebe das Leben
bio: Vasiliki Papathanasiou (born 23 August 1949), known professionally as Vicky Leandros, is a Greek-born German singer. She is the daughter of singer, musician, and composer Leandros Papathanasiou, also known as Leo Leandros and Mario Panas. In 1967, she gained international recognition after representing Luxembourg in the Eurovision Song Contest with the song "L'amour est bleu", which placed fourth and became a commercial success. She further advanced her career by winning the Eurovision Song Contest in 1972 with the song "Après toi"

title: Moskau
bio:
Dschinghis Khan (Pronounced "Jingis" Khan) was a German pop band, created in 1979 to compete in the Eurovision Song Contest. The name of the band is the German spelling of "Genghis Khan", and was chosen to fit the song of the same name, written and produced by Ralph Siegel with lyrics by Bernd Meinunger.

Appearing at the height of the disco boom and following on the heels of other German-produced bands such as Boney M., Arabesque, and Silver Convention

title: Anita
bio:
Konstantinos Kordalis (May 1, 1944 - July 2, 2019), known professionally as Costa Cordalis, was a Greek-German schlager singer. He became known in the 1970s with songs such as "Carolina, komm und steig' in das Boot heute nacht" and "Anna Lena". His greatest commercial success was the 1976 single "Anita".

Cordalis was born in central Greece and began playing guitar at the age of seven. At 16, he moved to Frankfurt am Main, where he learned German at the Goethe Institute.

title: Marina
bio:
Rocco Granata (b. August 16, 1938) is a Belgian singer, songwriter, and accordionist.

Granata was born in Figline Vegliaturo, Calabria, southern Italy; but his parents immigrated to Belgium when he was ten. Rocco's father was a coal miner, but after briefly working in the coal mines, Granata pursued music instead. He played accordion and toured Belgium with his band, "The International Quintet". He then released the song "Manuela" b/w "Marina" as a single in 1959; the B-side became an international smash hit

bio:

title: Marmor, Stein und Eisen bricht
bio:
Drafi Richard Franz Deutscher (born 9 May 1946 in Berlin-Charlottenburg; died 9 June 2006 in Frankfurt am Main) was a German singer, composer, and music producer. As a singer, he had a number-one hit in Germany in 1965 with "Marmor, Stein und Eisen bricht."

According to his own account, Drafi Deutscher came from the Jewish musical family Kálmán and grew up in poverty in Berlin with his grandmother. The well-known Hungarian operetta composer Emmerich Kálmán was said to be a great-uncle of Deutscher.

title: Ich bin wie du
bio:

title: Fiesta Mexicana
bio:
Rex Gildo (July 2, 1939 - October 26, 1999) was a German singer of schlager ballads who reached the height of his popularity in the 1960s and 1970s, selling over 25 million records and starring in film and television roles.

Gildo was born Ludwig Alexander Hirtreiter in Straubing, Bavaria and was a member of the Regensburger Domspatzen choir before going to acting school. His first performance was with the Munich Kammerspiele theatre group in 1956

title: Santa Maria
bio: Roland Kaiser (born on May 10, 1952 in Berlin) is a well known German songwriter and singer. He is also known as a great entertainer and his performances are very popular, not only in Germany, but also in Netherlands and Belgium. His most popular songs are "Santa Maria" (in 1980) and "Dich zu lieben" (1981).

title: 1000 Träume weit (Tornero)
bio: Anna-Maria Zimmermann was one of the contestants of the third season of the German version of Pop Idol, titled "Deutschland sucht den Superstar" (Germany searching for the Superstar). She made it into the group of the final ten competing on the big Saturday night shows. As such she recorded a song for the album of the finalists "Love Songs": "Wish You Were Here".

title: Am Tag, als Conny Kramer starb
bio: Juliane Werding is a German pop music singer-songwriter. Born in Essen in 1956, she had her musical breakthrough in the spring of 1972, and she has released numerous albums and singles since. She won the 'Golden Tuning Fork' in the 1980s. A devout Christian, she has co-authored the books "Tell me, Lord Jesus... and other interviews with people of the Bible" and "Whores, hypocrites, saints... interviews with people of the Bible". She also has a career as a therapist.

title: Ti Amo
bio: Howard Victor Carpendale (* January 14, 1946, in Durban, South Africa) is a German-South African Schlager singer and composer. He had his greatest single hits in German-speaking countries during the 1970s and 1980s, including Ti amo (1977) and Hello Again (1984). Over the course of his career, more than 25 million of his records were sold.

title: Im Wagen Vor Mir
bio: Henry Valentino begeistert Musikfans weltweit mit dem Track "Im Wagen Vor Mir". Ein absoluter Jukebox-Liebling im City Cafe Klagenfurt Fischl!

title: Sag mir wo du stehst
bio:
Born out of the Hootenanny Club and the Singebewegung (Singing Movement) Oktoberklub was a political activist's song group in the GDR (German Democratic Republic/ East Germany). The band derives its name from the october revolution and  establishment of the GDR.

Oktoberklub (also Oktober-Klub) sang international political songs (partially in Nachdichtungen), self-creations as well as traditional people and combat songs.. Apart from normal song evenings with mixed repertoire it led also revue-like arranged programs starting from 1971.

title: Du entschuldige - I kenn' Di
bio:
This tag can refer to two different artists:

1. Peter Cornelius (born January 29, 1951 in Vienna, Austria) is an Austrian pop-singer, guitarist and a former member of Enigma.

Offical homepage:
http://www.petercornelius.com/

2. Peter Cornelius, also known as Carl August Peter Cornelius, (24 December 1824 – 26 October 1874) was a German composer, writer about music, poet and translator. He was born and died in Mainz where his grave in the Hauptfriedhof survives.

title: Die Biene Maja
bio:
Karel Gott (July 14, 1939 - October 1, 2019) was one of the most successful and widely-known Czech singers. He is known as  "the golden throat of Prague" and "Sinatra of the East".

Born in Plzeň, Western Bohemia, Gott was initially trained as a mechanic; at the same time, he performed as an amateur singer in various Prague pubs of the late 1950s. In the 1960s he started to study singing at the Prague Conservatoire, his first short record Měsíční řeka (a Czech version of Henry Mancini's "Moon River") being issued in 1962.

bio:
Ibo Bekirovic, a German-Yugoslavian pop singer who became famous with the hit song "Ibiza". He died in the year 2000 in an automobile accident. 

Ibo Bakker a dutch singer/songwriter/troubadour performs as "Ibo" as well.

title: Tanze Samba mit mir
bio:
There are several artists with this name: 1. a German schlager singer, 2. an American harmonica player from Memphis, Tennessee.
---
1) Tony Holiday (February 24, 1951 - February 14, 1990) was a German pop singer and songwriter.

Born Rolf Peter Knigge in Hamburg, West Germany, Knigge began his career first as a textile businessman and fashion designer. In 1974 he received a record contract with Hans Bertram, who rechristened him "Tony Holiday". His first two singles met with little success.

album: Album: Musik Liegt In Der Luft - Vol.1
bio: Siw Malmkvist, born December 31, 1936 in Landskrona, Sweden, is a popular Swedish singer famous in Scandinavia and Germany. She competed in the Swedish Melodifestivalen many times, latest in 2004 together with Towa Carson and Ann-Louise Hanson, singing the song C'est la vie, which finished 10th. She represented Sweden at the international Eurovision Song Contest 1960 with Alla andra får varann and the former West Germany at the Eurovision Song Contest 1969, with the song Primaballerina.

title: Tränen lügen nicht
bio: Michael Holm (born Lothar Walter, 29 July 1943) is a German singer, musician, songwriter, and record producer, primarily known for performing Schlager music. He first appeared in the German hit parade in 1962 with the song "Lauter schöne Worte", but achieved his first major commercial success in 1969 with "Mendocino", a German-language adaptation of a song by the Sir Douglas Quintet. Released in September 1969, the single reached number three on the German charts for five weeks and sold over one million copies

title: Amsterdam
bio:
Cora is a Curitiba based band of dreampop/psychedelic music.

Kaíla - vocals and synth
Katherine - guitars and vocals

The EP "Não vai ter cora" was released in May/2017.
The LP "El Rapto" was released in April/2018.

title: Ein bisschen Frieden
bio:
Nicole may refer to several musical artists and bands using the same name.

1. Nicole is the stage name of Chilean singer Denisse Laval. She began her career as a child performer and achieved early commercial success with the album "Tal Vez me Estoy Enamorando". Her later album "Esperando Nada", produced by Tito Dávila of Enanitos Verdes, sold more than 75,000 copies in Chile. In 1997, she released "Sueños en Tránsito", produced by Gustavo Cerati, which established her wider recognition in Latin America.

title: Wann wird's mal wieder richtig Sommer
album: Album: Kult Schlager: Die bunte Schlagerwelt
bio: Rudi Carrell (19 December 1934 in Alkmaar, The Netherlands — 7 July 2006 in Bremen, Germany), born Rudolf Wijbrand Kesselaar, was a Dutch entertainer (singer / actor / presenter / producer). He worked as a television entertainer and hosted his own show. The Rudi Carrell Show ran first in the Netherlands, then in Germany for many years. Carrell was also a singer with a number of hits, and acted in several movies. His career began with the television show for AVRO "De bonte dinsdagavondtrein" (1955)

title: Schöner fremder Mann
bio:
Connie Francis (born Concetta Rosa Maria Franconero in Newark, New Jersey on 12 December 1937; died  16 July 2025) was an American pop singer remembered for her international hit songs such as "Who's Sorry Now?," "Where The Boys Are", and "Everybody's Somebody's Fool". She is estimated to have sold more than 200 million records worldwide. 

Born in Italian Down Neck (or Ironbound) neighbourhood of Newark, New Jersey, Francis was one of the most prolific

title: Du
bio:
Peter Maffay (born 30 August 1949 in the Romanian town of Braşov, German: Kronstadt ; real name: Peter Alexander Makkay) is a German musician.

In 1963, Maffay and his parents emigrated from the Siebenbürgen region in Romania to Germany, like other Transylvanian Saxons. In the same year, he started his first band, The Dukes. After completing his education and spending some time working for Chemigraphics, an art manufacturer, Maffay worked in clubs, where he distributed his music.

title: Er hat ein knallrotes Gummiboot
bio:
Wenche Synnøve Myhre (born February 15, 1947, in Kjelsås, Oslo) is a Norwegian singer and actress who has had a great number of hit songs since the 1960s in the Norwegian, German as well as the Swedish markets and languages.

She got her first recording contract with composer and producer Arne Bendiksen at an age of 13, when she won a talent contest in Oslo in 1960.

Myhre represented Germany in the Eurovision Song Contest in 1968 with the song 'Ein hoch der liebe'.

title: Von Hier Bis Unendlich
bio: Helene Fischer (born 5 August 1984) is a German singer and entertainer. Since her debut in 2005 she has won numerous awards, including 17 Echo awards, 4 "Die Krone der Volksmusik" awards and 3 Bambi awards. According to record certifications she has sold at least 15,000,000 albums (As of 11/18). In June 2014, her multi platinum 2013 album "Farbenspiel" became the most legally downloaded album by a German artist of all time, and her 2017 album "Helene Fischer (2017)" became the 2nd highest first week sales debut ever on the German Albums Charts.

title: Ein Bett im Kornfeld
bio:
Jürgen Drews is a German pop/schlager musician.

Essentially serving as Germany's answer to Tom Jones, Drews secured his spot in the annals of German pop music with his trashy schlager singles and his flamboyant showmanship. After putting in his time with Die Anderen and the Les Humphries Singers, Drews went solo and broke onto the German pop charts in 1976 with a country crossover hit entitled "Ein Bett im Kornfeld" (A Bed in a Cornfield). Drews managed to score some minor hits throughout the '80s

title: über den wolken
bio:
Reinhard Friedrich Michael Mey (born December 21, 1942, Berlin) is a German singer-songwriter, also known as a Liedermacher. In France he is known as Frédérik Mey.

And although he is known as a German singer, he has also recorded songs in Dutch. "Als de dag van toen" has become a favourite among Dutch fans. His song "Gute nacht Freunde"
is still the ending tune of the well known Dutch radio show "Met het oog op morgen". Every night a few minutes after eleven the song is played.

Entry for Luxembourg

Song title: Chansons Pour Ceux Qui S'aiment

title: Ich will keine Schokolade
bio:
Trude Herr war eine beliebte Schauspielerin, die über einige Jahre mit ihrem "Theater im Vringsveedel" das Kölner Publikum begeisterte. Hierbei kann sie für sich in Anspruch nehmen, mit ihren Theaterstücken das Volkstheater ins 20.Jahrhundert katapultiert zu haben. Herrs Theater verkörperter den Gegensatz zum klassischen Volkstheater, wie es im Millowitsch-Theater bis heute gespielt wird.
Berühmt wurde Trude Herr durch ihre Auftritte in deutschen Nachkriegs-Komödien wie "Drillinge an Bord" und "Ohne Krimi geht die Mimi nie ins Bett".

album: Album: Das Beste Von Rainhard Fendrich
bio: Rainhard Jürgen Fendrich (born 27 February 1955 in Vienna) is an Austrian singer, composer, entertainer, and actor. He is one of the most successful Austropop musicians. His lyrics are written in Viennese German, and he is very popular in Austria, but less in other German-speaking countries, and by far less known in non-German speaking ones. His song from 1990, I am from Austria (its lyrics are, except for this one line, in Viennese German) is still popular in Austria.

title: Schuld war nur der Bossa Nova
album: Album: Schuld war nur der Bossa Nova
bio:
There are 2 bands called Manuela.

1) London-based project of vocalist and songwriter Manuela Gernedel and multi-instrumentalist Nick McCarthy (of Franz Ferdinand / FFS).

“Slinky, understated dance-rock that shuffles along with echoing, escalating guitar lines and precise drums that snap with intent.” - Stereogum

Austrian-born Manuela Gernedel and her British husband Nick McCarthy first moved to Glasgow when Gernedel began studying painting at the Glasgow School of Art.

bio: Hoffmann & Hoffmann was a German singing and songwriting duo in the schlager genre. It consisted of Michael Hoffmann (* December 3, 1950, in Karlsruhe) and his brother Günther Hoffmann (* October 4, 1951, in Karlsruhe; † March 15, 1984, in Rio de Janeiro). The two brothers started making music together during their school years. Their first major hit, Himbeereis zum Frühstück, the German version of the Bellamy Brothers' hit Crossfire, led to an appearance on ZDF-Disco on September 17, 1977.

title: Michaela
bio:
Bata Illic (born 30 September 1939 in Belgrade) is a Serbian schlager singer. Illic studied English and Italian and later worked as a teacher. In 1963, he and his band secured a multi-month engagement at an American club in Bad Kissingen. This was followed by further performances at clubs in Bad Hersfeld, Fulda, and Berlin.

In 1967, Illic recorded his first album. His first chart successes came in 1968 with the songs Mit verbundenen Augen and Schuhe, so schwer wie ein Stein.

title: Ohne Dich (schlaf' ich heut Nacht nicht ein)

She is estimated to have sold more than 200 million records worldwide.

title: Wunder gibt es immer wieder
bio:
Katja Ebstein (born Karin Witkiewicz; 9 March 1945) is a German singer. She was born in Girlachsdorf, now Gniewków in Poland, and became known for songs including "Theater" and "Es war einmal ein Jäger". She was married to composer Christian Bruhn, who wrote many of her songs.

Ebstein represented Germany in the Eurovision Song Contest on three occasions, in 1970, 1971, and 1980. She also participated in the national selection "Ein Lied für Stockholm" in 1975 with the song "Ich liebe dich", finishing fifth.

He is also known as a great entertainer and his performances are very popular, not only in Germany, but also in Netherlands and Belgium. His most popular songs are "Santa Maria" (in 1980) and "Dich zu lieben" (1981).

title: Immer wieder sonntags
bio: Cindy & Bert was a German Schlager duo consisting of Jutta Berger (Cindy, born Gusenburger; 26 January 1948 in Völklingen, Saarland) and Norbert Maria Berger (Bert; 12 September 1945 in Völklingen, Saarland – 14 July 2012 in Düsseldorf). The duo gained popularity in the 1970s. Their most successful songs included "Immer wieder sonntags" (1973), "Spaniens Gitarren" (1974), and "Aber am Abend da spielt der Zigeuner" (1974). In the late 1980s, Jutta Berger also established a solo career under the name Cindy Berger.

title: Blau blüht der Enzian
bio:
Heino (born 13 December 1938 in Düsseldorf as Heinz Georg Kramm) is a German singer of popular music (Schlager) and traditional Volksmusik.

With his booming voice, bright blonde hair, and ever present sunglasses (due to Graves-Basedow disease), Heino is considered a national treasure in his native Germany, and an icon of kitsch in the English-speaking world - no doubt due to his resemblance to Andy Warhol and Bingo Rimér and the Latin American

title: Du hast den Farbfilm vergessen
album: Album: Du hast den Farbfilm vergessen
bio:
Catharina "Nina" Hagen (born 11 March 1955) is a German singer, songwriter, and actress, recognized for her theatrical vocal style and her prominence during the punk and Neue Deutsche Welle movements in the late 1970s and early 1980s. She has been referred to as "The Godmother of German Punk."

Hagen was born and raised in East Berlin, German Democratic Republic. She began her career as an actress, appearing in several German films alongside her mother, Eva-Maria Hagen.

title: Du schwarzer Zigeuner
bio: Vico Torriani (21 September 1920 - 26 February 1998) was a popular Swiss Schlagersinger, actor, TV showmaster, and author of cookbooks. Born Ludovico Oxens Torriani in Geneva, Switzerland, Vico grew up in St. Moritz. In the 1950s and early 1960s he starred in a dozen German film musicals and comedies, and he also hosted his own television show, "Grüezi, Vico". He sang in a dozen languages, including Italian, German, French, Romansch and Hebrew, and was fluent in six of them himself.

title: Lieb mich ein letztes Mal

title: Marleen
bio:
Marianne Rosenberg (born March 10, 1955, in West Berlin) is a German pop and schlager singer as well as a songwriter. She gained fame in the 1970s with hits like Mr. Paul McCartney, Er gehört zu mir, Lieder der Nacht, and Marleen. Rosenberg was born into an artistic family in Berlin and had a notable relationship with Ilja Richter in the late 1970s. She has a son, Max (born 1993), who is also a musician.

Rosenberg's father, Otto Rosenberg, was a Sinto and a survivor of the Porajmos, the genocide of the European Roma and Sinti during WWII.

title: Feuer Am Horizont
bio: Helene Fischer (born 5 August 1984) is a German singer and entertainer. Since her debut in 2005 she has won numerous awards, including 17 Echo awards, 4 "Die Krone der Volksmusik" awards and 3 Bambi awards. According to record certifications she has sold at least 15,000,000 albums (As of 11/18). In June 2014, her multi platinum 2013 album "Farbenspiel" became the most legally downloaded album by a German artist of all time, and her 2017 album "Helene Fischer (2017)" became the 2nd highest first week sales debut ever on the German Albums Charts.

title: Laura
bio:
Johannes Hendricus Maria "Jan" Smit (born December 31, 1985) is a Dutch pop music and schlager singer. He scored his first number one position in the Dutch charts with Ik zing dit lied voor jou alleen (I sing this song only for you) in 1997. Being 12 years of age back then, he was known as Jantje Smit.

Smit was discovered by Dutch middle of the road band BZN, who used his vocals in a duet called Mama. He has released several albums in Dutch and German since.

title: Barfuß im Regen
bio: Michael Holm (born Lothar Walter, 29 July 1943) is a German singer, musician, songwriter, and record producer, primarily known for performing Schlager music. He first appeared in the German hit parade in 1962 with the song "Lauter schöne Worte", but achieved his first major commercial success in 1969 with "Mendocino", a German-language adaptation of a song by the Sir Douglas Quintet. Released in September 1969, the single reached number three on the German charts for five weeks and sold over one million copies

title: Am Tag als Conny Kramer starb
bio: Juliane Werding is a German pop music singer-songwriter. Born in Essen in 1956, she had her musical breakthrough in the spring of 1972, and she has released numerous albums and singles since. She won the 'Golden Tuning Fork' in the 1980s. A devout Christian, she has co-authored the books "Tell me, Lord Jesus... and other interviews with people of the Bible" and "Whores, hypocrites, saints... interviews with people of the Bible". She also has a career as a therapist.

title: Rocky
bio: Frank Farian (born Franz Reuther in Kirn, Germany, on 18 July 1941; died in Miami, Florida, on 23 January 2024) was a German schlager singer and record producer who founded the 1970s disco-pop group Boney M., the dance music groups Far Corporation, the Latin pop band No Mercy, and the pop band Milli Vanilli. He frequently created vocal groups in which the publicised members merely lip-synced to songs sung by session members. Farian owned the record label MCI and several subsidiaries.

bio:
There are multiple artists named Wind:

1. Wind was a German psychedelic rock band influenced by 1960s West Coast styles. Their debut album "Seasons" (1971) has been described as an early example of progressive rock, featuring extended and complex compositions within a heavy, psychedelic framework. Their second album, "Morning" (1973), presented a more direct and conventional rock approach. The band did not achieve significant commercial success and disbanded in 1973. Drummer Lucky Schmidt subsequently continued his career with the band Aera.

title: So bist du
bio:
Peter Maffay (born 30 August 1949 in the Romanian town of Braşov, German: Kronstadt ; real name: Peter Alexander Makkay) is a German musician.

In 1963, Maffay and his parents emigrated from the Siebenbürgen region in Romania to Germany, like other Transylvanian Saxons. In the same year, he started his first band, The Dukes. After completing his education and spending some time working for Chemigraphics, an art manufacturer, Maffay worked in clubs, where he distributed his music.

title: Ich bin von Kopf bis Fuß auf Liebe eingestellt

Marie Magdelene Dietrich von Losch (aka Marlene) was born in Schöneberg bei Berlin, Germany on December 27, 1901. Her father was an army officer who had served in the Franco-Prussian War. Because of his constant absences due to his army duties, Marlene and the family had to rely on themselves. After he died, while she was 11, Marlene's mother married Eduard von Losch and he adopted the Dietrich children. 

Marlene was known in school for her "bedroom

title: Allein in Griechenland
bio:
Nicole may refer to several musical artists and bands using the same name.

1. Nicole is the stage name of Chilean singer Denisse Laval. She began her career as a child performer and achieved early commercial success with the album "Tal Vez me Estoy Enamorando". Her later album "Esperando Nada", produced by Tito Dávila of Enanitos Verdes, sold more than 75,000 copies in Chile. In 1997, she released "Sueños en Tránsito", produced by Gustavo Cerati, which established her wider recognition in Latin America.

title: Gianna
bio:
Wolfgang "Wolle" Petry (born Franz Hubert Wolfgang Remling, 22 September 1951) is a German schlager musician and songwriter from Cologne, Germany. In 1997, he was recognized as the leading German-language musician in terms of chart performance for that year, largely due to the success of his album "Alles".

Petry received the Echo award for "Best Folk/Pop Artist National/International" consecutively from 1997 to 2001. He was also awarded the Goldene Stimmgabel for Most Successful German Solo Pop Act in 1996

title: Hier kommt Kurt
bio:
Frank Kurt Zander (born 4 February 1942 in Berlin) is a German Schlager and pop singer, presenter, actor, and voice actor. He achieved his breakthrough in 1974 with the single "Der Ur-Ur-Enkel von Frankenstein". In the same year he released "Ich trink’ auf dein Wohl, Marie", followed by "Oh, Susi (Der zensierte Song)" in 1976.

Zander reached his commercial peak in 1981 when, under the pseudonym Fred Sonnenschein, he achieved a number-one hit with "Ja, wenn wir alle Englein wären".

title: Oh, wann kommst du?
bio: Daliah Lavi (Daliah Lewinbuk, Hebrew: דליה לביא‎‎; 12 October 1942 - 3 May 2017) was an Israeli actress, singer, and model. With the decline of her film career, Lavi was discovered by record producer Jimmy Bowien and began a successful schlager singing career in Germany, with hits such as "Oh, wann kommst du?", "Willst du mit mir gehn?" and "C'est ça, la vie (So ist das Leben)". She recorded German-language covers of a.o. Melanie Safka's Look What They've Done to My Song

title: Die Bouzouki klang durch die Sommernacht
bio: Vasiliki Papathanasiou (born 23 August 1949), known professionally as Vicky Leandros, is a Greek-born German singer. She is the daughter of singer, musician, and composer Leandros Papathanasiou, also known as Leo Leandros and Mario Panas. In 1967, she gained international recognition after representing Luxembourg in the Eurovision Song Contest with the song "L'amour est bleu", which placed fourth and became a commercial success. She further advanced her career by winning the Eurovision Song Contest in 1972 with the song "Après toi"

bio: Christian Anders, an Austrian Schlagersänger,  was born in 1945 in Bruck an der Mur in Austria. His original name is Antonio Augusto Schinzel-Tenicolo. His father was Italian and, after the war ended, the family left Austria for Sardinia where he lived until they moved to Offenbach am Main in Germany when he was 10. After finishing at school he started training as an electrician but his keen interest in music soon led him to form a band which he called 'Christian Anders and the Tonics'.

title: Du bist nicht allein
bio:
Roy Black (January 25, 1943–October 9, 1991) was a German Schlager singer and actor who appeared in several musical comedies and starred in the 1989 TV series Ein Schloß am Wörthersee.

Born Gerhard Höllerich in Straßberg near Augsburg, Roy Black attended the gymnasium in Augsburg and, aged 20, founded the rock and roll band Roy Black and His Cannons. His stage name derived from his black hair and his idol Roy Orbison.

Roy Black and His Cannons achieved some local fame and were offered a contract with Polydor Records.

title: Reif für die Insel
bio:

1. Peter Cornelius (born January 29, 1951 in Vienna, Austria) is an Austrian pop-singer, guitarist and a former member of Enigma.

Offical homepage:
http://www.petercornelius.com/

2. Peter Cornelius, also known as Carl August Peter Cornelius, (24 December 1824 – 26 October 1874) was a German composer, writer about music, poet and translator. He was born and died in Mainz where his grave in the Hauptfriedhof survives.

title: Zwei kleine Italiener
bio:
Cornelia Froboess (born October 28, 1943) is a German actress and a teen idol of the 1950s and early '60s.

During that time, Froboess appeared in many musical films, especially after the rock 'n' roll wave had hit Germany. In those comedy films, she would often portray the typical Berliner Göre (girl from Berlin) who craves for independence from her all-too-strict parents.

As Conny Froboess, she had her first hit in 1951, aged eight, with a song written by her father.

title: Heut' Abend hab' ich Kopfweh
bio: Ireen Sheer (born 25 February 1949) is a German-English singer. She had her first major hit in 1970 with Hey Pleasure Man. She had a top five hit on the German singles chart with "Goodbye Mama" in 1973. She went on to finish fourth at the Eurovision Song Contest 1974 representing Luxembourg, sixth at the Eurovision Song Contest 1978 representing Germany, and thirteenth at the Eurovision Song Contest 1985 representing Luxembourg again.

title: Ich bin verliebt in die Liebe
bio:
There are at least 2 artists with the name Chris Roberts. 1) A German singer and actor (active 1968-2017), 2) A Welsh classical guitarist (active 2014 - present).

1) Chris Roberts (Christian Klusáček; March 13, 1946 - July 2, 2017) was a German Schlager singer and actor. He was married to the singer and actress Claudia Roberts, they often performed on stage together.

Born in Munich-Schwabing, Germany, Roberts represented Luxembourg as a part of a sextet in the Eurovision Song Contest 1985 in which they sang "Children, Kinder, Enfants".

bio: The Flippers (1964–1965: Dancing Band, 1965–1969: Dancing Show Band) were a German Schlager band that performed in various lineups – initially six members, later three – between 1963 and 2011. With over 40 million records sold, they are among the most commercially successful music groups in Germany. Their best-known songs include "Weine nicht, kleine Eva", "Sha La La, I Love You", "Die rote Sonne von Barbados", "Lotosblume", and "Wir sagen danke schön".

He had his greatest single hits in German-speaking countries during the 1970s and 1980s, including Ti amo (1977) and Hello Again (1984). Over the course of his career, more than 25 million of his records were sold.

title: Da sprach der alte Häuptling
bio:
Donald Edgar "Gus" Backus (September 12, 1937 – February 21, 2019) was an American singer and a member of The Del-Vikings.

After he was stationed as an Airman in the U.S. Air Force at Wiesbaden Air Base in 1957, Backus had to leave The Del-Vikings but he later became a popular singer of Schlager music in 1960s West Germany. He sang all his songs in German with an American accent. 19 of his songs between 1960 and 1967 made the charts, including eight Top Ten hits.

bio:
There are two artists with this name.

The first Andreas Martin is: http://www.brainwashed.com/andreas/

The second Andres Martin is a lutenist who was born in Frankfurt am Main in Germany.

He studied guitar with Mario Sicca (Musikhochschule Stuttgart) and Ruggero Chiesa (Conservatorio di Musica Giuseppe Verdi), as well as Anglo–Saxon and Romance Languages and Philosophy at the University of Heidelberg.

At the Schola Cantorum in Basel, he worked under the tutelage of lutists Eugen Dombois, Hopkinson Smith and Peter Croton.

title: Jenseits von Eden
bio: Nino de Angelo (born Domenico Gerhard Gorgoglione on 18th December 1963 in Karlsruhe, Germany) is a German actor and singer of Italian ancestry, being best known for his environmental protest song "Jenseits von Eden" (English: Beyond Eden") that was an interntional #1 hit. His first album, titled 'Junges Blut' (English: 'Young Blood'), came out in March 1983. Playing a melodic pop/rock style, his large popularity in the 80s in both West Germany and East Germany continued for several years, but his career faced some changes later on.

title: Verdammt ich lieb' Dich
album: Album: Du knallst in mein Leben
bio: Matthias Reim (born on 26 November 1957 in Korbach, Hesse) is a German pop/rock singer-songwriter. Performing an often dramatic, emotional form of 'schlager' music, his first single, titled "Verdammt, ich lieb’ Dich" (English: "Damn, I love you"), came out in 1990. The internationally famous single sold over 2.5 million copies and was at top of the charts in Germany for 16 weeks. No single had kept that position for so long since 1971, with Reim sharing this distinction with Boney M.

title: Schenk' mir diese eine Nacht
bio: Brunner & Brunner was an Austrian schlager duo consisting of the brothers Karl Brunner (born 28 February 1955 in Graz), known as Charly, and Johann Brunner (born 19 March 1958 in Tamsweg), known as Jogl. Active between 1992 and 2010, they regularly charted in Germany and Austria with their singles and albums.

title: Schöne Maid
bio:
Tony Marshall (born Herbert Anton Hilger, née Bloeth; 3 February 1938 - 16 February 2023) was a German schlager and opera singer. He became known in 1971 with the hit song "Schöne Maid" and later appeared in several comedy productions and numerous television programmes.

Marshall was born in Baden-Baden and trained as an opera singer in Karlsruhe, graduating in 1965.

Instead of pursuing a career in opera, he entered popular music and achieved his first commercial success in 1971 with "Schöne Maid".

title: Adios Amor
bio: Andy Borg (born November 2, 1960 in Vienna) is an Austrian Schlager singer. He lives in the Köln area, and has been constantly recording music since his debut album Adios Amor was released in 1982.

There are at least 4 artists with this name:

1) Doris Nefedov, born Treitz, (May 19, 1942 - July 31, 1969) was a German singer who performed under the stage name Alexandra.

Nefedov was born on May 19, 1942 in Heydekrug, Memelland (today: Silute, Lithuania). Her first hit single, "Zigeunerjunge" ("gypsy boy"), came out in 1967; several more followed, including "Sehnsucht" ("yearning"), "Schwarze Balalaika" ("black balalaika") and "Mein Freund, der Baum" ("my friend, the tree").

In 2010, he legally changed his name to Udo Jürgens Bockelmann.

bio:
Nicole may refer to several musical artists and bands using the same name.

1. Nicole is the stage name of Chilean singer Denisse Laval. She began her career as a child performer and achieved early commercial success with the album "Tal Vez me Estoy Enamorando". Her later album "Esperando Nada", produced by Tito Dávila of Enanitos Verdes, sold more than 75,000 copies in Chile. In 1997, she released "Sueños en Tránsito", produced by Gustavo Cerati, which established her wider recognition in Latin America.

title: Was ich Dir sagen will
bio:
Jürgen Udo Bockelmann (30 September 1934 – 21 December 2014), generally known as Udo Jürgens, was an Austrian composer and singer of popular music whose career spanned over 50 years. He won the Eurovision Song Contest 1966 for Austria, composed close to 1,000 songs, and sold over 104 million records. In 2007, he additionally obtained Swiss citizenship. In 2010, he legally changed his name to Udo Jürgens Bockelmann.

He is credited with broadening

title: Schön ist es auf der Welt zu sein
bio:
Roy Black (January 25, 1943–October 9, 1991) was a German Schlager singer and actor who appeared in several musical comedies and starred in the 1989 TV series Ein Schloß am Wörthersee.

Born Gerhard Höllerich in Straßberg near Augsburg, Roy Black attended the gymnasium in Augsburg and, aged 20, founded the rock and roll band Roy Black and His Cannons. His stage name derived from his black hair and his idol Roy Orbison.

Roy Black and His Cannons achieved some local fame and were offered a contract with Polydor Records.

title: Weine nicht kleine Eva
album: Album: 35 Jahre - Ein Leben für die Zärtlichkeit
bio: The Flippers (1964–1965: Dancing Band, 1965–1969: Dancing Show Band) were a German Schlager band that performed in various lineups – initially six members, later three – between 1963 and 2011. With over 40 million records sold, they are among the most commercially successful music groups in Germany. Their best-known songs include "Weine nicht, kleine Eva", "Sha La La, I Love You", "Die rote Sonne von Barbados", "Lotosblume", and "Wir sagen danke schön".

title: Ich bin ein Mann
bio:
Ted Herold born 09.09.1942 in Berlin-Schoeneberg.
Rock'n'roll Singer. While he sings in German language he starts Rock'n'Roll to become famouse in Germany.
1958 first record "Ich brauch keinen Ring"/"Lover Doll" and his first #1 for three weeks. For his Version from "Moonlight" he gets the "Goldene Stimmgabel"

title: Speedy Gonzales
bio:
Rex Gildo (July 2, 1939 - October 26, 1999) was a German singer of schlager ballads who reached the height of his popularity in the 1960s and 1970s, selling over 25 million records and starring in film and television roles.

Gildo was born Ludwig Alexander Hirtreiter in Straubing, Bavaria and was a member of the Regensburger Domspatzen choir before going to acting school. His first performance was with the Munich Kammerspiele theatre group in 1956

title: Morgen
bio: Incorrect tag for Ivo Robić.

title: Der Mann im Mond
album: Album: Best Of The Rock N' Roll Years
bio:
Donald Edgar "Gus" Backus (September 12, 1937 – February 21, 2019) was an American singer and a member of The Del-Vikings.

After he was stationed as an Airman in the U.S. Air Force at Wiesbaden Air Base in 1957, Backus had to leave The Del-Vikings but he later became a popular singer of Schlager music in 1960s West Germany. He sang all his songs in German with an American accent. 19 of his songs between 1960 and 1967 made the charts, including eight Top Ten hits.

title: Heimweh
bio:
Freddy was born on September 27th, 1931 in Vienna, Austria. In his youth he loves the circus, but in 1950 he chooses for music and starts singing in the bars from Hamburg.
In 1952 he joins an international competition for the first time in Belfast, Ireland, where he wins the first prize.
However it takes till 1954 before he records his first songs, mainly songs about life at sea and some country music.
He soon extends his career by learning to act, and he will join several movies in Germany.

title: Sehnsucht (Das Lied der Taiga)
bio:
There are at least 4 artists with this name:

1) Doris Nefedov, born Treitz, (May 19, 1942 - July 31, 1969) was a German singer who performed under the stage name Alexandra.

Nefedov was born on May 19, 1942 in Heydekrug, Memelland (today: Silute, Lithuania). Her first hit single, "Zigeunerjunge" ("gypsy boy"), came out in 1967; several more followed, including "Sehnsucht" ("yearning"), "Schwarze Balalaika" ("black balalaika") and "Mein Freund, der Baum" ("my friend, the tree").

title: Mit 17 hat man noch Träume
bio:
Peggy March (born Margaret Annemarie Battavio on March 8, 1948, Lansdale, Pennsylvania) is an American pop music singer. She was discovered at age thirteen singing at a wedding and was introduced to the record producer duo Hugo & Luigi. They gave her the nickname Little Peggy March because of her 4'10" height, the fact that she was thirteen, the first record she did with them was "Little Me", and her birthdate was in March.

In April 1963, her single "I Will Follow Him" had soared to number one on U.

title: Feierabend
bio: Peter Alexander (* June 30, 1926 as Peter Alexander Ferdinand Maximilian Neumayer in Vienna; † February 12, 2011 in Vienna; also known under the pseudonym Alexander Neuenhauser) was an Austrian singer, actor, pianist, impersonator, and entertainer. From the mid-1950s to the mid-1990s, he was one of the most popular entertainers in the German-speaking world. Between 1952 and 1972, he appeared in around fifty film comedies and forty of his own TV shows, and he recorded approximately 120 albums.

title: Die Fischer Von San Juan
bio: Andy Borg (born November 2, 1960 in Vienna) is an Austrian Schlager singer. He lives in the Köln area, and has been constantly recording music since his debut album Adios Amor was released in 1982.

title: Für alle
bio:
There are multiple artists named Wind:

1. Wind was a German psychedelic rock band influenced by 1960s West Coast styles. Their debut album "Seasons" (1971) has been described as an early example of progressive rock, featuring extended and complex compositions within a heavy, psychedelic framework. Their second album, "Morning" (1973), presented a more direct and conventional rock approach. The band did not achieve significant commercial success and disbanded in 1973. Drummer Lucky Schmidt subsequently continued his career with the band Aera.

title: Bye Bye Belinda
album: Album: Das muss doch Liebe sein

title: Feuer
bio: Ireen Sheer (born 25 February 1949) is a German-English singer. She had her first major hit in 1970 with Hey Pleasure Man. She had a top five hit on the German singles chart with "Goodbye Mama" in 1973. She went on to finish fourth at the Eurovision Song Contest 1974 representing Luxembourg, sixth at the Eurovision Song Contest 1978 representing Germany, and thirteenth at the Eurovision Song Contest 1985 representing Luxembourg again.

title: Cowboy und Indianer
bio:
Popular German singer, who is specialized in the styles Discofox / Europop, started his career in 1997. Before that he worked as an insurance salesman. 
His releases are a modern form of discofox comparing to the 80´s or early 90´s - a bit faster, simple melodies and lyrics, all track sounds similar as their build up and the used instruments and arrangements are often the same. If he would sing in Italian Language you could say his releases sound a little bit like Italo-Dance.

title: Lili Marleen
bio: Lale Anderson (born March 23, 1905 in Bremerhaven, Germany – died August 29, 1972 in Vienna, Austria) was a German singer and songwriter, who is best known for being the first ever artist to record the song "Lili Marleen" on August 1, 1939 (originally titled "Lied Eines Jungen Wachtpostens"). Anderson initially only sold 700 copies of her recording. The song became tremendously popular on both sides of the front during World War II, when it was frequently played by Radio Belgrad in the then-occupied Yugoslavia.

bio: Helene Fischer (born 5 August 1984) is a German singer and entertainer. Since her debut in 2005 she has won numerous awards, including 17 Echo awards, 4 "Die Krone der Volksmusik" awards and 3 Bambi awards. According to record certifications she has sold at least 15,000,000 albums (As of 11/18). In June 2014, her multi platinum 2013 album "Farbenspiel" became the most legally downloaded album by a German artist of all time, and her 2017 album "Helene Fischer (2017)" became the 2nd highest first week sales debut ever on the German Albums Charts.

title: Fremder Mann
bio:
Marianne Rosenberg (born March 10, 1955, in West Berlin) is a German pop and schlager singer as well as a songwriter. She gained fame in the 1970s with hits like Mr. Paul McCartney, Er gehört zu mir, Lieder der Nacht, and Marleen. Rosenberg was born into an artistic family in Berlin and had a notable relationship with Ilja Richter in the late 1970s. She has a son, Max (born 1993), who is also a musician.

Rosenberg's father, Otto Rosenberg, was a Sinto and a survivor of the Porajmos, the genocide of the European Roma and Sinti during WWII.

title: Butterfly
bio:
Danyel Gérard (born Gérard Daniel Kherlakian, 7 March 1939, Paris) is a French pop singer and composer.

Gérard was born in Paris to an Armenian father and an Italian mother, but grew up mainly in Rio de Janeiro. In 1953 he returned again to Paris and became a choir boy at Notre Dame. Following this he played in the rock and roll band The Dangers. In 1958 he made his first recordings: "Viens" (a cover of the Kalin Twins' hit "When") and "D'où reviens-tu Billy Boy" (adapted from Dorothy Collins' "Where Have You Been

title: Sommer in der Stadt
bio:
Wolfgang "Wolle" Petry (born Franz Hubert Wolfgang Remling, 22 September 1951) is a German schlager musician and songwriter from Cologne, Germany. In 1997, he was recognized as the leading German-language musician in terms of chart performance for that year, largely due to the success of his album "Alles".

Petry received the Echo award for "Best Folk/Pop Artist National/International" consecutively from 1997 to 2001. He was also awarded the Goldene Stimmgabel for Most Successful German Solo Pop Act in 1996

title: Afrika
album: Album: Das beste aus 40 Jahren Hitparade
bio:
Ingrid Peters (born April 19, 1954 in Dudweiler, Saarland) is a German singer.

She represented Germany in in the Eurovision Song Contest 1986 held in Bergen. Her song, Über die Brücke geh'n was placed eight. Peters had made previous attempts to represent Germany. In 1979 she performed a song called Du bist nicht frei and in 1983 was runner-up in the national contest with Viva la mamma. Peters was a special guest on the German national contest in 1987 and 2007

title: I Bin A Bayrisches Cowgirl
bio:
Nicki (born Doris Andrea Hrda on November 2 ,1966  in the town of Plattling (in the Deggendorf  district of Bavaria , Germany ) is a German pop singer and composer. She currently lives with her life partner and their two daughters in Plattling.
Career
Hrda grew up with three brothers and two sisters in Plattling. In 1982, she was at a talent competition when producer/songwriter Gog Seidl discovered her and signed her to a record contract. Her stage name of "Nicki" was thought up by pop star Sandra  (of Enigma  fame.)

title: Ich hab' geträumt von dir
bio: Matthias Reim (born on 26 November 1957 in Korbach, Hesse) is a German pop/rock singer-songwriter. Performing an often dramatic, emotional form of 'schlager' music, his first single, titled "Verdammt, ich lieb’ Dich" (English: "Damn, I love you"), came out in 1990. The internationally famous single sold over 2.5 million copies and was at top of the charts in Germany for 16 weeks. No single had kept that position for so long since 1971, with Reim sharing this distinction with Boney M.

title: Ganz oder gar nicht
bio:

title: Aufrecht geh'n
bio:
Mary Roos (born Rosemarie Schwab on January 9 1949) is a German singer and actress.

Schwab was born in Bingen. At the age of nine, she recorded her first song "Ja die Dicken sind ja so gemütlich" as Die kleine Rosemarie. It was not a major commercial success.

In 1958, she also appeared in the film 'Die Straße'. Over the following years she would release many records and she also appeared in many singing competitions. Her first chart success came in 1965, when the single "Geh nicht den Weg" peaked at #36.

title: Seemann, deine Heimat ist das Meer
Sommernacht in Rom
bio: G. G. Anderson (born 4 December 1949 as Gerd Grabowski in Eschwege) is a German Schlager singer, composer, and music producer. Among his best-known songs are "Mama Lorraine" and "Sommernacht in Rom". As a composer, he has written more than 1,000 songs in German and English for artists including Roland Kaiser, Boney M., Andrea Jürgens, Mary Roos, Jürgen Drews, Karel Gott, and the Wildecker Herzbuben.

title: Erst ein Cappuccino
bio:
Kristina Bach (born Kerstin Bräuer on April 7, 1962, in Mettmann) is a German pop singer, songwriter, lyricist, and music producer. After completing school, she trained in singing, dance, piano, guitar, and acting. She also worked as a TV presenter before starting her music career.

Bach was discovered as a singer in the early 1980s, releasing her first single “Donna Maria” in 1983. She gained initial success in 1984 with a cover of the 1960s hit “Heißer Sand.

title: Ein Festival der Liebe
bio:
Eurovision Song Contest 1976 - The Hague
Entry for Luxembourg

Performer: Jürgen Marcus
Song title: Chansons Pour Ceux Qui S'aiment
Song writer(s): Vline Buggy, Fred Jay
Song composer(s): Jack White 

Sang in Position:  5
Final Position:  14
Total Points:  17

After being host of the 1975 Eurovision Song Contest, Sweden withdrew from the contest as Swedish TV thought that the contest had become too commercial. They were also afraid of winning again and having to host the expensive show again.

title: Alt wie ein Baum
bio:
The Puhdys were a German rock band founded in 1969, although an earlier version of the group, later referred to as the "Ur-Puhdys", had existed since the mid-1960s. They were among the most prominent rock bands in the German Democratic Republic (GDR) and remained active after German reunification. Following the announcement of their dissolution in February 2014, the band gave its final official concert in Berlin in January 2016.

The final line-up consisted of Dieter "Maschine" Birr

title: Amore Mio
bio: Roland Kaiser (born on May 10, 1952 in Berlin) is a well known German songwriter and singer. He is also known as a great entertainer and his performances are very popular, not only in Germany, but also in Netherlands and Belgium. His most popular songs are "Santa Maria" (in 1980) and "Dich zu lieben" (1981).

title: Souvenirs
bio: William McCreery Ramsey (born April 17, 1931 in Cincinnati, Ohio) is a German-American singer, journalist and actor. His claim to fame were several German pop songs (Schlager), such as "Ohne Krimi geht die Mimi nie ins Bett" and "Zuckerpuppe(aus der Bauchtanzgruppe)".

title: Azzurro
album: Album: Peter Rubin: Seine großen Erfolge
bio: Peter Rubin, born Peter Kohlhuber (* 5 May 1942 in Mährisch-Ostrau, Reichsgau Sudetenland, today Czech Republic), is a German singer, presenter, songwriter, and guitarist. Rubin completed a commercial apprenticeship after finishing school. He then studied economics at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich. In the 1960s, he founded the band Pemagos with friends. In 1966, his first record, Wieder daheim (Homeward Bound), was released. In 1967, he represented Germany at the International Song Contest in Sopot, Poland.

title: Eloisa
album: Album: Einfach leben - Best Of - Dankeschön für all die Jahre
bio:
Roger Whittaker (born Roger Henry Brough Whittaker in Nairobi, Kenya, on 22 March 1936; died 13 September 2023 ) was a British singer-songwriter and musician remembered for his eclectic mix of folk music and popular song, his baritone singing voice, and his trademark whistling skills. 

He became widely known for his version of "Wind Beneath My Wings" (1982), as well as his own compositions "Durham Town (The Leavin')" (1969) and "I Don't Believe in If Anymore" (1970)

title: Sugar Baby
bio: Peter Siegfried Krausenecker, known as Peter Kraus, born on 18 March 1939 in Munich, is a German-Austrian actor and singer. The son of Austrian director and cabaret artist Fred Kraus, he spent his youth alternating between Munich and Salzburg, where his father ran a small theatre. During his school years, Peter Kraus took singing lessons, acting classes, and tap-dancing courses and began his acting career in the mid-1950s with the role of Johnny in Das fliegende Klassenzimmer (1954), based on the novel by Erich Kästner.

title: Schreib es mir in den Sand
bio:
Frank Schöbel (eigentlich Frank-Lothar Schöbel; * 11. Dezember 1942 in Leipzig) ist ein deutscher Musiker. Er [war] einer der erfolgreichsten Schlagersänger der DDR.

Als zweiter Sohn einer Opernsängerin wurde sein musikalisches Talent schon früh entdeckt. Als Siebenjähriger besuchte er den Vorbereitungslehrgang für den Thomanerchor. Doch statt in den Chor einzutreten, interessierte er sich mehr für Popmusik. Er absolvierte eine Mechanikerlehre, seine Musikerlaufbahn begann er im Erich-Weinert-Ensemble der Nationalen Volksarmee.

bio:
There are multiple artists with this name.

1.  Bruce Low is a Suriname born Dutch Schlager and Gospel singer born 26 March 1913, died 3 March 1990 in München.

2. Bruce Low is an Australian singer-songwriter was born on 13th December 1970 in Namibia, and his family moved to Australia in 1977. He learnt to play the drums at age 12 and picked up the guitar a few years later, heavily influenced by folk artists like Bob Dylan and Cat Stevens. More recently he has learnt some keyboards as well and, on some recent recordings, plays all instruments.
